$comment: Shared cross-implementation interop test vector for the e2e-auth.v1 Noise suite. Every implementation of the encrypted rig-control channel (cqdx @cqdx/noise [TS], panino NoiseSession [TS/RN], pancetta [Rust snow]) MUST reproduce this handshake BYTE-FOR-BYTE before it is trusted to interoperate. This is the single oracle that closes the JS<->Rust interop risk called out in the remote-rig build (ADR-0002 / e2e-auth.v1).

primitives.kdf: Noise HKDF built from HMAC-BLAKE2s (NOT HKDF-SHA256): temp_key = HMAC(ck, ikm); out1 = HMAC(temp_key, 0x01); out2 = HMAC(temp_key, out1 || 0x02); out3 = HMAC(temp_key, out2 || 0x03)

implementation_notes.nonce_encoding: The 96-bit ChaCha20-Poly1305 nonce is 4 zero bytes followed by the 64-bit Noise message counter `n` in little-endian (Noise spec 12.3). The counter is per-CipherState, starts at 0, and advances once per encrypt and once per decrypt.
implementation_notes.handshake_payloads: Each handshake message carries an (optionally empty) payload that is encrypted-and-hashed; in these vectors the payloads are non-empty (Austrian-economist names) and the prologue is non-empty ('John Galt' = 4a6f686e2047616c74).
implementation_notes.transport_ad: Post-handshake transport messages use EMPTY associated data.
implementation_notes.ik_pattern: Pre-message `<- s` (responder static, mixHash'd by both parties). msg1 tokens `e, es, s, ss` (initiator->responder). msg2 tokens `e, ee, se` (responder->initiator). After msg2 both split(): initiator { send: c1, recv: c2 }, responder { send: c2, recv: c1 }.
implementation_notes.key_fields: init_static / init_ephemeral / resp_static / resp_ephemeral are 32-byte X25519 PRIVATE keys (hex). init_remote_static is the responder's PUBLIC static key (hex). Derive public halves with X25519 base-point mult; e.g. pub(resp_static) == init_remote_static.

message_directions: messages[0]: handshake msg1, initiator -> responder (eph + enc-static + enc-payload)
messages[1]: handshake msg2, responder -> initiator (eph + enc-payload)
messages[4]: transport, initiator -> responder (initiator send nonce = 1)
messages[5]: transport, responder -> initiator (responder send nonce = 1)

source.name: cacophony test vectors
source.note: The canonical cacophony vector set that the Rust `snow` crate (pancetta's Noise implementation) validates against. Vendored here so all three repos share one byte-level oracle.

conformance.cqdx: PASS - @cqdx/noise (hand-rolled on @noble/curves+ciphers+hashes) reproduces this handshake byte-for-byte (msg1, msg2, handshake_hash, and all transport messages incl. nonce=1). cqdx Step 3b-1.
conformance.panino: PASS - panino NoiseSession conformance-tested byte-for-byte against this vector (dispensa Q-0011, 2026-06-28).
conformance.pancetta: PASS - snow 0.9.6 (Noise_IK_25519_ChaChaPoly_BLAKE2s, fixed_ephemeral_key_for_testing_only) reproduces THIS entry byte-for-byte: msg1, msg2, handshake_hash (both initiator and responder), and all four transport messages incl. the nonce=1 pair (messages[4]/[5]). Confirmed by pancetta 2026-06-30 with a standalone snow harness against this exact vector; will land as a drift-guarded conformance test when the agent Noise layer is built.
